title: Hybrid Join Timeout 0x80004005 — Fixed by Windows Update
description: A Microsoft Entra hybrid join Autopilot deployment hit error 0x80004005 — a documented build-dependent known issue, resolved by KB5065789 (25H2, build >= 26200.6725), KB5065426 (24H2, build >= 26100.6584), and KB5070312 (23H2, build >= 22631.6276). Knowledge source: docs/agent/autopilot-ztd-diagnostics.md (error-code map).
author: Autopilot Monitor
explanation:
This is a **Microsoft Entra hybrid join** deployment that hit error **0x80004005** — a documented, build-dependent Autopilot known issue where hybrid deployments time out.

The issue is fixed by Windows cumulative updates:

- Windows 11 **25H2**: KB5065789 — fixed from build **26200.6725**
- Windows 11 **24H2**: KB5065426 — fixed from build **26100.6584**
- Windows 11 **23H2**: KB5070312 — fixed from build **22631.6276**

Compare this session's OS build (see the Session Info card / os_info event) against the fixed-build threshold for its version. If the device booted with an older build, updating the deployment image (or installing quality updates during OOBE) resolves the timeout. Note that 0x80004005 is a generic failure code (E_FAIL) — if the build is already at or above the fixed threshold, investigate the Intune Connector for Active Directory and domain-controller connectivity instead.

remediation.title: Update the image to the fixed build
steps: Determine the session's OS build from the Session Info card (e.g. 26100.4500).
Refresh the deployment image with the current cumulative update, or enable quality updates during OOBE in the ESP profile.
If the build already contains the fix: check Intune Connector for AD health, domain-controller reachability from the client network, and hybrid-join prerequisites.

relatedDocs.title: Microsoft Learn: Windows Autopilot known issues — hybrid join 0x80004005
